name: Kirov Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: purity of moral principles, kindness, modesty, and snowy winter
hope, joy, health, fertility of the earth, and forest wealth
fidelity, honesty, impeccability, and the Vyatka River uniting the population on its banks
the heraldic shield in the center
details of the coat of arms

description: A wide white field with two narrow stripes - green over blue - along the bottom, charged in the center with the red heraldic shield of the Kirov Oblast coat of arms.
history: Adopted in 2003 (current design); features the historic shield of Vyatka in the center of a predominantly white field.

features.symbolism: wide white field with narrow green and blue stripes at the bottom; white for purity and snowy winter, green for hope and forest wealth, blue for the Vyatka River.
the central heraldic shield from the oblast's coat of arms, showing a hand emerging from a cloud holding a bow with arrow.
